LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT KELLY CLARK'S MOTION TO REOEPN DISCVOERY AFTER JUDICAL ARBITRATION AWARD IS DENIED AND PLAINTIFF JULIE ALBIN'S REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS IS DENIED. BASED ON PLAINTIFF'S REPRESENTATION THAT SHE HAS PRODUCED ALL MEDICAL AND WAGE LOSS DOCUMENTATION IN HER POSSESSION, CUSTODY AND CONTROL, DEFENDANT HAS FAILED TO SHOW GOOD CAUSE FOR REOPENING DISCOVERY. NOTWITHSTANDING DEFENDANT'S LACK OF GOOD CAUSE TO SUPPORT THE GRANTING OF THE MOTION, DEFENDANT HAD SUBSTANTIAL JUSTIFICATION FOR BRINGING THE MOTION AND THUS MONETARY SANCTIONS ARE DENIED. THE PREVAILING PARTY SHALL SUBMIT A [PROPOSED] WRITTEN ORDER THAT TRACKS THE COURT'S RULING, VERBATIM. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: JOSE RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: NOT REPORTED. (D302)

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT KELLY CLARK'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT AND DEFAULT JUDGMENT {CCP 473.5}. THE COURT CONFIRMS THE TENTATIVE RULING: GRANTED. MS. CLARK HAS SHOWN GOOD CAUSE PER CCP 473.5 TO SET ASIDE THE DEFAULT AND THE TWO AND A HALF MONTH "DELAY" IN FILING THIS MOTION IS NOT A BASIS TO DEPRIVE HER OF BEING ABLE TO DEFEND THIS CASE ON THE MERITS. GRANTING THE MOTION IS STRONGLY SUPPORTED BY CALIFORNIA'S POLICY OF DECIDING CASES ON THEIR MERITS AND PLAINTIFF HAS NOT SHOWN THAT HER ABILITY TO PROVE HER CASE WOULD BE PREJUDICED BY THE GRANTING OF THE MOTION. PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR FEES AND COSTS IS DENIED SINCE THERE IS NO BASIS TO BELIEVE THAT MS. KELLY OR HER COUNSEL IN ANY WAY ACTED IMPROPERLY OR NEGLIGENTLY. THE PREVAILING PARTY SHALL SUBMIT A [PROPOSED] WRITTEN ORDER THAT TRACKS THE COURT'S RULING, VERBATIM. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: JOSE RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: SHERRY L. SAWYER, CSR NO. 5976

Defendant Kelly Clark'S Motion To Reoepn Discvoery After Judical Arbitration Award
Off Calendar Dec-05-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

Defendant Kelly Clark's Motion To Reoepn Discvoery After Judical Arbitration Award is DENIED and plaintiff Julie Albin's request for monetary sanctions is denied. Based on plaintiff's representation that she has produced all medical and wage loss documentation in her possession, custody and control, defendant has failed to show good cause for reopening discovery. Notwithstanding defendant's lack of good cause to support the granting of the motion, defendant had substantial justification for bringing the motion and thus monetary sanctions are denied. The prevailing party shall submit a [proposed] written order that tracks the Court's ruling, verbatim.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Not Reported. (D302)

Off Calendar Nov-10-2016 Continued To Dec-21-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion For Proving Up Default Judgment
Off Calendar Oct-20-2016 Off Calendar

MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
Off Calendar Oct-06-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Byron M. Purcell of Ivie McNeill & Wyatt (213.489.0028) appears telephonically as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party, Julie Albin

No other appearance is made in this matter.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT KELLY CLARK's MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT AND DEFAULT JUDGMENT {CCP 473.5} is GRANTED. Ms. Clark has shown good cause per CCP 473.5 to set aside the default and the two and a half month "delay" in filing this motion is not a basis to deprive her of being able to defend this case on the merits. Granting the motion is strongly supported by California's policy of deciding cases on their merits and plaintiff has not shown that her ability to prove her case would be prejudiced by the granting of the motion. Plaintiff's request for fees and costs is denied since there is no basis to believe that Ms. Kelly or her counsel in any way acted improperly or negligently. The prevailing party shall submit a [proposed] written order that tracks the Court's ruling, verbatim.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Sherry L. Sawyer, CSR No. 5976, email: ssawyer@sftc.org. (D302)
